The '''''Munifex''-class light cruiser''', colloquially known as the '''Class 1000 cruiser''', was a [[Light cruiser/Legends|light cruiser]] design from [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], used by the [[Trade Defense Force]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==History==
The ''Munifex''-class was originally used by the [[Trade Defense Force]] and gained its nickname from the number of ships initially ordered.<ref name="TEGTW" />

Around [[214 BBY]], a group of [[Transport/Legends|transport]] businesses in the [[Rseik sector/Legends|Rseik Sector]] decided to join the Trade Federation. They were burdened by pirates, and the Defense Force sent a [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] including a [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser|''Captor''-class heavy munitions cruiser]] and two ''Munifex''-class cruisers to stop the pirates.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

Relatively heavily-armed for its size, but not boasting the fastest [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], it was not as prominent in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] as ships like the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class cruiser]] and the [[Star Galleon-class frigate|''Star Galleon''-class frigate]], and may have been found primarily in relatively low-priority sectors.<ref name="GG6" />

For instance, in the remote [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]], the Class 1000 cruiser [[Intrepid (Munifex-class)|''Intrepid'']] served as the guard ship for the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] prison in the [[Gesaril system/Legends|Gesaril system]].<ref name="GG6" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Munifex was first named in the ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''.
